# Arguments after the first positional are rejected, not silently dropped:
# Go flag parsing stops at the first non-flag argument, so a stray arg
# would otherwise swallow the trailing -move and perform a copy.
! exec sflit -source a.go -sink b.go -regex '^Filter' stray.go -move
stderr 'unexpected argument'
! exists b.go
grep 'func FilterA' a.go

-- a.go --
package p

func FilterA() {}

func Other() {}
